name: Hel's Cloak
description: Hel's Cloak, also known as the Helm of Darkness or Helm of Terror, is a legendary artifact associated with the Norse goddess Hel, ruler of the underworld, Helheim. This cloak, often described as being dark or black, possesses the power to render the wearer invisible or to shroud them in shadow, effectively concealing them from sight. It is a potent symbol of Hel's dominion over the hidden realms and her ability to control the passage between worlds. The cloak's origins are shrouded in mystery, with some accounts suggesting it was crafted by dark dwarves or bestowed upon Hel by the Allfather Odin himself to aid her in her duties. Its significance lies in its ability to grant access to the unseen and to provide protection from detection, making it a valuable tool for those seeking to traverse the realms of the dead or to operate in secret. The cloak's power is not merely limited to physical invisibility; it can also obscure the wearer's intentions and thoughts, making them appear harmless or insignificant to others. This potent combination of physical and mental concealment makes Hel's Cloak a formidable artifact, feared and respected throughout the nine realms. The cloak represents not just invisibility, but also the hidden aspects of death and the unknown, reinforcing Hel's role as a gatekeeper to the afterlife. It is said that the cloak is kept in Helheim, accessible only to those who have earned Hel's favor or possess the knowledge and strength to navigate the treacherous paths leading to her domain. The precise material composition of the cloak remains a mystery, but it is often speculated that it is woven from the shadows of the dead or the darkness of the void, imbued with the chilling essence of Helheim itself. The cloak’s power is amplified during the darkest hours of the night and is weakened in the presence of strong light or divine energy. To wield Hel’s Cloak is to embrace the power of concealment and the secrets of the underworld, a dangerous path that few dare to tread.
subtitle: Veil of Invisibility and Shadow
origin: Crafted by dark dwarves or bestowed by Odin.

keyMyths.title: Hel's Dominion
keyMyths.description: Hel uses her cloak to move unseen within her realm and to observe the happenings in the mortal world. It allows her to maintain control over the dead and to prevent unauthorized crossings between worlds.

keyMyths.title: Concealment in Ragnarok
keyMyths.description: During Ragnarok, it is theorized that Hel may use her cloak to conceal her forces and to launch surprise attacks against the gods. This could give her an advantage in the final battle.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology.
